heading: Acceptance of gifts of property
label: 3175 Acceptance of gifts of property
text: The Administrator of General Services, and the United States Postal Service where that office is concerned, may accept on behalf of the Federal Government unconditional gifts of property in aid of any project or function within their respective jurisdictions.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)317540:298a., title IV, § 404, .June 16, 1949, ch. 21863 Stat. 199The words “Administrator of General Services” are substituted for “Federal Works Administrator” because of section 103(a) of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 (ch. 288, ), which is restated as section 303(c) [303(b)] of the revised title. The words “United States Postal Service” are substituted for “Postmaster General” because of section 6() of the Postal Reorganization Act (, ). The words “real, personal, or other” are omitted as unnecessary.63 Stat. 380Public Law 91–37584 Stat. 783o
